capabilityCandidates.label: macOS synthetic input (mouse + keyboard via enigo)

engineerRoles.name: Frontend Engineer
engineerRoles.systemPrompt:
You are a senior frontend engineer specializing in React and TypeScript. Focus on component architecture, type safety, accessibility, and pixel-perfect UI implementation. Follow the project's existing patterns and conventions. Prefer composition over inheritance, keep components small and focused.

## Agent operating contract (8 modules)

You run inside Project Manager as an autonomous agent, not a one-shot chat. Follow this architecture:

1. **LOOP (循環控制)** — Keep iterating: plan → act → observe → adjust until the task is done or you must stop. Do not stall after a single reply if work remains.

2. **HOOKS (Pre / Post / Stop)** — Respect project hooks and skills (investigate before guessing fixes; ship only after verify baseline). Run pre-checks before risky edits; leave logs and state clean on stop.

3. **STATE M. (狀態機)** — Know which phase you are in (explore, implement, test, review). Stay inside the working scope paths injected with this dispatch; do not wander into unrelated directories.

4. **EVALUATOR (評估器)** — Judge your own output: typecheck, tests, lint, acceptance criteria. If verification fails, treat it as a problem you detected — fix or report, do not pretend success.

5. **STOP POLICY (停 / 繼續)** — Continue while there is clear forward progress and budget remains. Stop when the goal is met, you are blocked with a concrete question, or verification cannot pass after reasonable retries.

6. **SUBAGENT (子代理委派)** — Delegate focused subtasks (explore codebase, run tests, investigate CI) instead of doing everything in one bloated turn. Merge sub-results back into the main thread.

7. **CONTEXT (注入 / 壓縮)** — Use the role system prompt, skills list, feature spec, and scope as ground truth. Prefer citing repo files over assumptions; compress long history into decisions and remaining work.

8. **TOOLS / MCP (工具與協議)** — Use assigned tools and MCP integrations for filesystem, terminal, browser, and external services. Never invent tool results; surface tool errors explicitly.

**Self-awareness:** If you hit an error, contradiction, missing context, or failing check, say so plainly — the human should not have to guess that you are stuck.

engineerRoles.name: Backend Engineer
engineerRoles.systemPrompt:

engineerRoles.name: Full-stack Engineer
engineerRoles.systemPrompt:

engineerRoles.name: QA Engineer
engineerRoles.systemPrompt:
You are a senior QA engineer. Focus on writing comprehensive tests that cover happy paths, edge cases, and error scenarios. Prefer integration tests over pure mocks. Think about what could go wrong and write tests that would catch regressions. Treat EVALUATOR and STOP POLICY as your primary job: every dispatch should end with evidence (passing tests or a clear failure report).

engineerRoles.name: DevOps Engineer
engineerRoles.systemPrompt:
You are a senior DevOps engineer. Focus on build pipelines, containerization, deployment automation, and system reliability. Prefer declarative configuration. Make deployments repeatable and rollbacks easy. Prioritize HOOKS and STOP POLICY: pipelines must fail loudly, and rollbacks must be safe.

engineerRoles.name: 開發者體驗專員
engineerRoles.systemPrompt:
You are a senior Developer Experience (DX) specialist. Focus on reducing friction across the developer workflow — clear CLI/UI affordances, actionable error messages, smooth onboarding, well-structured docs, and ergonomic defaults. Audit features through the lens of a first-time user: identify rough edges, missing guardrails, and confusing terminology. Prefer small, polish-oriented improvements that compound over time.

features.name: Project dashboard and progress tracking
features.notes: Removed non-existent path __tests__/progressDashboard.aggregations.test.tsx from evidencePaths.

features.name: Chat assistant interface
features.notes: Includes chat page, panel, message rendering, and input interactions.

features.name: AI assistant and agent workflows
features.notes: Workflow orchestration for AI-driven assistance and agent task handling.

features.name: Capabilities registry and state machine
features.notes: Central capability modeling, registration, and progression logic.

features.name: Local adapters for IDEs and agents
features.notes: Adapter layer for spawning or integrating local IDEs and agent CLIs such as Claude Code and Codex.

features.name: API bridge and execution orchestration
features.notes: Dry-run execution planning and bridge orchestration for dispatching actions.

features.name: GitHub repository and issue synchronization
features.notes: Corrected locatedSection from invalid /api/github to module candidate app/api/github.

features.name: Keys and environment secret management
features.notes: Provider detection, env parsing, validation, and keychain-backed storage.

features.name: Documentation site and public guides
features.notes: Public documentation rendering backed by generated site manifests and markdown content.

features.name: Project scaffolding and setup
features.notes: Creates and normalizes project structures from templates or disk.

features.name: Scanning, ingestion, and project analysis
features.notes: Runs project scans, selects models, validates results, and reviews extracted structure.

features.name: Project files and workspace views
features.notes: Workspace-centric views for browsing projects and related project state.

features.name: Plugins → Connect Sheet
features.notes: Integration Hub scan/test actions are decoupled per inventory sheet; System Installed Apps now scans and tests independently.

features.name: Engineer Capabilities: Eyes / Voice / Hands / Recording
xmux Coding Tool Sidebar Entry
features.notes: Add one xmux sidebar item, rename the cmux-backed coding-tool adapter to xmux, and provide an interactive terminal/browser/split workspace while documenting cmux-derived capabilities and future integration boundaries.

features.name: xmux Pane Unification, Stable Browser State & Smooth Resize
features.notes: Follow-up to F27. PaneShell primitive extracted; both TerminalPaneGroup and BrowserPane derive from it. BrowserPane is now multi-tab and per-workspace, so 'New Browser' appends instead of overwriting. Resize dividers stay smooth across iframe and xterm via a fixed-position drag overlay + rAF-coalesced state updates. 21 new tests across pane-shell / workspace-tabs / resize files; 570 total passing. Remaining: manual verification on a Tauri desktop build before flipping to done.

features.name: AI Assistant Capability Upgrade
features.notes: Upgrade AI Assistant to match/exceed OpenClaw capabilities. Enhanced system prompt, tool execution, slash commands v2, cross-session memory, streaming UX polish. Fixed chat API key bridging: server routes now accept client-loaded keys from providerKeyStore.

features.name: xmux Native Browser URL Chrome Stability
features.notes: Fix xmux native browser URL chrome, focus black-pane, orphan overlay, split-resize ghosting, and browser-pane close cleanup. Native browser webviews are parked during pane/sidebar resize, restored from latest bounds after drag end, and destroyed from the layout-level close path before block removal.

features.name: Built-in MCP Servers & Execution Method Column
features.notes: Added out-of-the-box MCP server templates for Exa Search, Context7 Docs, and Grep.app. Added an 'Execution Method' column in the Integrations Hub table with custom styled badges, multi-language support (English, Traditional Chinese, Simplified Chinese, Japanese), and full type safety.

features.name: Network Discovery Plan
features.notes: P0 + nmap install scripts. Fixed US-05: checking nmap on Passive LAN auto-switches Active + CIDR hint (no Run dead-end). Manual Tauri: F33-M02/M03 pending.

features.name: Xmux AI Assistant Block and Browser Tools
features.notes: Removed pane-toolbar AI Assistant tab; Select Element sends DOM payload to docked Assistant; Console mirrors native webview logs; CSS Inspector now renders selected DOM tree, Design tab, CSS tab, box model, class list, and computed styles.

features.name: Agent Workflow DAG Control Plane
features.notes: WorkflowRun / WorkflowNodeRun state helpers, Dispatch DAG template picker, sidecar persistence, and AI Assistants Workflow Runs inspection are implemented. F35 now initializes run/node state, saves WorkflowRun JSON under .project-manager/workflow-runs, keeps worker sessions isolated, and surfaces persisted DAG state in the Console.

features.name: Performance Baseline, Route Splitting & xmux Smoothness
features.notes: Measured performance checkpoint focused on reducing inactive route code loaded through MainClient, preserving deep-link route props, and preparing targeted xmux smoothness follow-ups before broad dead-code cleanup.

features.name: Windows Font Zoom Shortcuts and Global Font Scaling
features.notes: Actual Size reset and View menu zoom actions added; Tauri/Windows manual validation pending

features.name: API Key Quick-Validate Panel
features.notes: Quick-validate panel with manual key input, format check, and /api/keys/validate integration

features.name: User-Controlled Plugin Install, Catalog Mirror, and Startup
features.notes: Plugin catalog mirror, autostart toggles, startup script gating, and dependency resolver are implemented; manual new-machine sidecar checks remain.

features.name: Launcher Profile Manifest and Environment-Aware Aux Pages
features.notes: Launcher profile manifest, aux-page reachability policies, profile samples, and shell/tests are implemented; installer/app packaging follow-up remains.

features.name: Terminal Operational Boundaries
features.notes: AI assistant shell whitelist/blacklist policy with Rust enforcement

features.name: Chat Runtime Security Boundary Migration
features.notes: Migrate AI Assistant chat provider calls behind secure runtime boundaries and remove renderer key exposure

features.name: Company Standards Gates One-Click Execution
features.notes: Per-gate Run and Run blocking gates wired in Tauri via standardsGates registry and spawnAgent; browser dev disables Run. Manual Tauri smoke pending.

features.name: Execution Policy Integration
features.notes: Standards gates use full policy stack; F43 bypass removed. Manual Tauri smoke pending.

features.name: Tiered Verification Workflow
features.notes: Split ship verification into quick, landing, and scheduled governance gates.

features.name: PM Mobile Voice Remote Control
features.notes: Phase 0 guarded mobile/channel run requests implemented; desktop approval path next

features.name: Supabase Cloud Auth and Developer Runner Storage Architecture
features.notes: Added Supabase cloud auth runbook covering env, RLS, membership, route guards, and runner readiness.

features.name: Self-hosted Supabase PM System Installer
features.notes: Added PM System Installer runbook covering roles, profiles, commands, secrets, doctor, backup, restore, and upgrade policy.

features.name: Development Dependency Graph Columns and Dispatch Guards
features.notes: Add upstream/downstream dependency tracking for Development sheet and prepare dispatch scheduling guards

features.name: Keys 三工作表重構：store v2 + arenaRunner + promotion 管線
features.notes: 5 phases; Phase 0 = P0 safety-net tests (no impl change). Plan approved 2026-06-10.

features.name: Project Dispatch Assistant
features.notes: Build a cross-discipline Project Dispatch Assistant that turns raw project work into role-ready packages, actor recommendations, risks, approvals, and executive summaries.

features.name: Project Workflow Loop Engine
features.notes: Completed F52 core: cross-discipline PM workflow loop engine model, software and construction quality templates, review-first run state, structured handoffs, evidence ledger, workflow events, persistent run store, chat /workflow decision package, scorecard blocking, stop policy, and human approval gates. Software Engineering Loop is the first template, not the product boundary.

plannedTestScope: unit: Project Workflow graph projection
integration: AI Assistants Workflow Runs graph rendering
integration: Workflow Runs save action creates and reloads a Project Workflow sidecar
manual E2E: /ai_assistants/workflow-runs browser smoke
risks: Existing Workflow Runs tab is agent-workflow based and must migrate without route regression.
Graph UI can become crowded; first slice avoids drag/drop and zoom.
Prompt/tool/memory metadata may need inferred labels until templates grow first-class fields.
metadata.scope: Upgrade AI Assistants Workflow Runs tab into a review-first Project Workflow graph execution console with an explicit UI save action.

features.name: Workflow Graph Execution Console
features.notes: F53 graph canvas console is complete: run browser, node graph, prompt/tool/memory/handoff/evidence inspector, approval gates, /workflow-save chat persistence, and a Workflow Runs UI Save workflow run action that creates review-first Project Workflow sidecars without executing agents or tools. Verified with focused unit/integration tests, browser smoke, Next dev issues check, and green verify:baseline.

plannedTestScope: unit: Project Workflow execution mode and draft policy
unit: graph projection of execution drafts
unit/store: dry-run execution request package creation and persistence
unit/store: execution request review gate and approval transition
unit/store: executor consumption policy for approved and unresolved packages
unit/store: dry-run executor handoff record for ready and blocked policy decisions
unit/store: dry-run executor run result record for completed and blocked policy decisions
unit/integration: Integrations Hub execution request queue rows
unit/integration: Integrations Hub execution handoff/run record audit rows
component: Execution Requests detail inspector review gate and payload visibility
component: Execution Requests detail inspector executor gate visibility
component: Execution Requests detail inspector Record executor handoff action and Execution Records read-only audit inspector
component: Execution Requests detail inspector Run dry-run executor action and Execution Records runner result visibility
component/manual E2E: approve execution request package without running command
integration: Workflow Runs Start node draft controls
manual E2E: /ai_assistants/workflow-runs, /integrations-hub/workflow-execution-requests, and /integrations-hub/workflow-execution-records browser smoke

Run-level auto mode must not bypass node risk, approval gates, or future Integration Hub policy.
Existing F53 listener/root fixes are dirty in the worktree and must be preserved.
metadata.scope: Add guarded execution draft creation and run-level auto mode to Workflow Runs without real actor/tool execution.

features.name: Workflow Execution Drafts & Run-Level Auto Mode
features.notes: Implemented F54 guarded execution-control slices: run-level execution mode controls, start-node execution draft creation, auto-safe Start node auto-request policy, draft graph projection, Workflow Runs Start node UI, Run draft request lifecycle, Integration Hub dry-run executor candidate resolver, injected cross-discipline executor registry support, command mapping executor metadata, editor fields, registry mapper, and Workflow Runs registry-aware request persistence, durable dry-run execution request package queue, default review_required policy gate, pure approved_for_executor transition for future runner handoff, pure executor consumption policy that blocks unapproved or unresolved packages, dry-run executor handoff records for ready and blocked policy decisions, dry-run executor run result records for completed and blocked policy decisions, shared dry-run runner adapter contract, blocked-package adapter bypass, persistent project-workflow-execution-records audit sidecars, Integration Hub Execution Records sheet, Record executor handoff action, Run dry-run executor action, visible executor gate decisions in the Execution Requests inspector, Integrations Hub Execution Requests sheet, read-only package inspector, explicit Approve for executor action that writes approved packages without running commands, browser dev sidecar reload support, sidecar persistence, and browser smoke. Real agent/tool execution remains out of scope until a reviewed Integration Hub executor is added.

features.name: SLO-Aware LLM Provider Routing
features.notes: Slices 1–3 delivered: SLI/SLO routing, Keys inference probe, Settings LLM Router Health. verify:baseline green 2026-06-21. Scan quorum deferred to future slice.

plannedTestScope: unit: lib/llm-router error taxonomy, SLO config, SLI window, health score, candidate ranking
unit: lib/keys probeModelSelection, validationProbe
unit: src-tauri llm_router_sli observation recording, pruning, SLO gate, ranking
unit: src-tauri llm_router_probe streaming probe + record_probe_sli
integration: call_llm_routed records deployments and emits skipped_slo attempts
integration: Keys validation path probes provider and writes probeResult + baseline SLI
manual: llm-router-state.json persistence after Tauri routed call and key validation
risks: Cold-start providers may be over-used before SLI samples accumulate.
Gemini probe uses E2E as TTFT proxy (no streaming TTFT yet).
Over-aggressive SLO gates could skip all candidates; must fall through with clear errors.
Router state file growth; observation cap per deployment required.
metadata.scope: Slice 1–3: SLI collection, SLO gates, health ranking in call_llm_routed; Keys inference probe; Settings LLM Router Health UI. Future: scan quorum.
